info.title: Style
info.description: A customizable dotnet tool that helps maintain a consistent C# coding style.

commands.name: format
commands.description: Formats C# files according to a defined coding style.

options.name: --path
options.description: The directory containing files to recursively format. Default: "<path>".

options.name: --style
options.description: Whether to format code using the 'dotnet format' formatter to run code style analyzers and apply fixes. Default: "True".

options.name: --analyzers
options.description: Whether to format code using the 'dotnet format' formatter to run third party code style analyzers and apply fixes. Default: "True".

options.name: --whitespace
options.description: Whether to format code using the 'dotnet format' formatter to run whitespace formatting. Default: "False".

options.name: --csharpier
options.description: Whether to format code using the 'CSharpier' opinionated formatter. Note: If formatting with --whitespace, this option must be disabled to avoid conflicts as they both handle whitespace formatting. Default: "True".

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The output verbosity level. Choices: "Quiet", "Normal", "Verbose". Default: "Normal".

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verifies that C# files comply with the defined coding style.

options.name: --path
options.description: The directory containing files to recursively verify the coding style compliance of. Default: "<path>".

options.name: --style
options.description: Whether to verify code complies with the coding style used by the 'dotnet format' formatter for code style analyzer settings. Default: "True".

options.name: --analyzers
options.description: Whether to verify code complies with the coding style used by the 'dotnet format' formatter for third party code style analyzer settings. Default: "True".

options.name: --whitespace
options.description: Whether to verify code complies with the coding style used by the 'dotnet format' formatter for whitespace settings. Default: "False".

options.name: --csharpier
options.description: Whether to verify code complies with the coding style used by the 'CSharpier' opinionated formatter. Note: If verifying with --whitespace, this option must be disabled to avoid conflicts as they both handle whitespace formatting. Default: "True".

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The output verbosity level. Choices: "Quiet", "Normal", "Verbose". Default: "Normal".
